failure_reason: Cited blocks p026_c0007 ('Off-Board Fare Collection') and p029_c0007 ('Off-board fare payment at all stations') mention the treatment but do not state any connection to the First Avenue/Second Avenue corridor. Neither block contains 'First Avenue', 'Second Avenue', 'corridor', or any spatial identifier. The relation relies entirely on document-level co-occurrence (the presentation is about First/Second Avenue SBS), which the instructions explicitly prohibit: 'co-occurrence in a document is not a relation.'

Cited blocks p026_c0008 and p029_c0008 both read only 'New low-floor, three-door buses' — neither mentions the First Avenue/Second Avenue corridor, M15, SBS, or any location. The connection is purely document-level co-occurrence, which is not a relation under the review rules.

Cited blocks p006_c0011 ('Within a ¼ mile:') and p006_c0012 ('537,000 residents') do not name the First Avenue/Second Avenue corridor or any corridor at all. 'Within a ¼ mile' of what is never stated in the cited blocks. The connection to corridor_first-second-avenues-brt depends entirely on knowing these blocks appear on the same page (p006) as the corridor description — that is co-occurrence, not a stated relation.

Same failure as rel-004: cited blocks p006_c0011 and p006_c0013 do not mention the First Avenue/Second Avenue corridor. '78% of households do not own a car' contains no corridor identifier. The relationship is inferred from page-level co-occurrence only.

Same failure as rel-004 and rel-005: cited blocks p006_c0011 and p006_c0014 do not reference the First Avenue/Second Avenue corridor. '57% of residents commute by transit' lacks any corridor identifier. The connection is co-occurrence on the M15 SBS overview page, not a statement in the blocks themselves.

Only one block (p029_c0009) is cited: 'Over six miles of new or improved bike lanes'. The raw text names neither the project nor M15 SBS. No heading/context block (like p029_c0005 used in rel-012) is provided to establish which project this metric belongs to. The connection to project_first-second-ave-sbs is pure co-occurrence on an un-cited page, which is explicitly not a relation.

Block p006_c0009 raw text is just 'Bus speeds have slowed' — it does not mention M15, any route identifier, or SBS. The connection to route_m15-sbs depends entirely on the un-cited assertion that this block appears on an M15 SBS page (p006_c0004). Co-occurrence in a document is not a relation.

Block p006_c0010 raw text is just 'Ridership has been decreasing' — it does not mention M15, any route identifier, or SBS. The connection to route_m15-sbs depends entirely on un-cited page context. Co-occurrence in a document is not a relation.

Block p030_c0006 raw text: 'To allow buses to serve school, conversion of 109 th St to two-way is proposed' — does not name the First Avenue/Second Avenue SBS project, M15, or any project identifier. The connection to project_first-second-ave-sbs is not stated in the cited block text.

Block p030_c0005 raw text: 'School buses currently load and unload on Second Av in front of J.H.S. 117 in future curbside bus lane' — references a 'future curbside bus lane' implying a planned project but does not name which project (First Avenue/Second Avenue SBS, M15, etc.). The connection to project_first-second-ave-sbs is not stated in the cited block text.
